title: 第 11 節
en: Agni, Vayu or Vi- Sarawyu (end of night, dyut, and Surya).
Vasatya, a son of Ushas, p. 608, and Nasatya.
28. Sadhyas. Vasus. Va^ins. Devapatnis (the wives of the gods).
Samudra (sea). Dadhyan {Rishi). Atharvan (Rishi). Manu (Rishi). Adityas (the sons of Aditi). Sapta Rishayah (the seven Rishis).

The chief work of Aditya is the lifting of moisture, and whatever deed is most pre-eminent, may be supposed to be done by him. The gods praised together with him areifandramas,Vayu, Samvatsara (the year).
We are also informed that whenever there is any reference in a verse to autumn, when the metre is Anush^ubh, the Stoma Ekavimsa, the Saman Vah%a, we may conclude that the verse is addressed to Agni.
When the season is winter, the metre Pankti, the Stoma Trinava, and the Saman #akvara, the probability is that the verse is intended for Indra, as dwelling in the air.

When the season is Sisira. (spring), the metre Ati£Mandas, the Stoma Trayastrimsa, the Saman Raivata, the verse is supposed to refer to Aditya, as dwelling in the sky. All this is artificial, but interesting, as giving the systematised views of later Brah manic theologians.
This gives us the following scheme : —
Agni. Worlds : Earth Libations : Morning Seasons : Spring (and Autumn) Metres : Gayatri (and Anush^ubh) Stomas : Trivrit (and Ekaviwwa)
Indra. AiiNoon Summer (and Winter) Tristaubh (and Pankti) Pa?>7cadasa (and Triwava)
Aditya. Sky Third Kains (and /Sisira) (?agati (and AtiftMandas) Saptadasa (and Trayastrimsa)
